description_long: This object features a large, hyperboloid concrete cooling tower structure with a wide base tapering inward and then flaring outward at the top. The upper surface is covered with a grassy green area, from which three tall wind turbines with slender white towers and three-bladed rotors emerge. The turbines are spaced apart, each anchored into the green surface, and their blades are oriented in different directions. The base of the tower is encircled by a series of evenly spaced, thin vertical supports or rods. There are no visible power lines, insulator stacks, or electrical crossarms, and the structure appears to combine elements of both power generation and cooling. No warning signage or electrical equipment is visible on the exterior.
description: A large concrete cooling tower with a grassy top supports three white wind turbines, encircled at the base by thin vertical rods.
description_view_0: The cooling tower is seen with two wind turbines clearly visible and a third partially obscured, showing the grassy top and the encircling rods at the base.
description_view_1: All three wind turbines are visible, with their blades oriented in different directions, and the green top surface is prominent.
description_view_2: The tallest wind turbine is most prominent, with the other two turbines partially visible behind it, and the tower's curved shape is clear.
description_view_3: The three wind turbines are evenly spaced and fully visible, highlighting the symmetry of the green top and the base's rod encirclement.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: No crossarms are present; the structure supports only wind turbines on its top surface.
attribute_configuration_of_attached_powe: No power lines are attached; the structure is solely topped with wind turbines and lacks visible electrical wiring.
